title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, leading to potential data encryption, service disruptions, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) and rotating access credentials.
Conduct a comprehensive audit of cloud infrastructure to identify and patch vulnerabilities, including misconfigurations and exposed endpoints.
Implement robust backup and disaster recovery plans, ensuring backups are stored offline or in a separate, secure environment.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: A critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability has been reported in PTC Windchill PDMlink...
description:
A critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability has been reported in PTC Windchill PDMlink and PTC FlexPLM. The vulnerability may be exploited through the deserialization of untrusted data.   *  This advisory also applies to all CPS versions
  *  The identified vulnerability also impacts Windchill and FlexPLM releases prior to 11.0 M030

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12569.

title: ThingsBoard contains a prototype pollution vulnerability which may lead to arbitrary code...
description: ThingsBoard contains a prototype pollution vulnerability which may lead to arbitrary code execution within a sandboxed context by a user who can log in to the affected product with the tenant administrator privilege (TENANT_ADMIN).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The prototype pollution vulnerability in ThingsBoard could allow an attacker with tenant administrator privileges to execute arbitrary code within a sandboxed environment. This could lead to unauthorized access, data manipulation, or disruption of services, potentially compromising the integrity and availability of the system.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patch or update provided by the ThingsBoard development team to address CVE-2026-53676.
Restrict tenant administrator privileges to only trusted users and regularly review user access permissions.
Monitor system logs for unusual activity, particularly from tenant administrator accounts, and implement intrusion detection mechanisms.

title: In SignalRGB versions prior to 1.3.7.0, seven of the thirteen IOCTL handlers dereference the...
description: In SignalRGB versions prior to 1.3.7.0, seven of the thirteen IOCTL handlers dereference the SystemBuffer pointer without first verifying that it is non-NULL. Sending an IOCTL with an empty input buffer causes a NULL pointer dereference, resulting in a kernel crash.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8050.

title: marimo before 0.23.9 contains a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability in the notebook page...
description: marimo before 0.23.9 contains a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability in the notebook page that allows unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary JavaScript by exploiting improper escaping of single quotes in the file query parameter reflected into an inline JavaScript string literal. Attackers can craft a malicious link with a payload beginning with __new__ to bypass the 404 check and inject JavaScript into the page, which executes without Content-Security-Policy restrictions in the o

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the context of the victim's browser. This could lead to data theft, session hijacking, or other malicious actions, especially if sensitive information is handled on the affected notebook page.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to marimo version 0.23.9 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement a Content-Security-Policy (CSP) to restrict the execution of inline JavaScript.
Conduct a security review of other input handling mechanisms to ensure proper escaping and sanitization.

title: In SignalRGB versions prior to 1.3.7.0, the \\.\SignalIo device object is created without an...
description: In SignalRGB versions prior to 1.3.7.0, the \\.\SignalIo device object is created without an explicit SDDL security descriptor and without FILE_DEVICE_SECURE_OPEN. This results in overly permissive default access control, allowing any authenticated local user to obtain a handle to the device and issue privileged IOCTLs.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8049.

title: Tinyproxy through 1.11.3, fixed in commit ff45d3b, fails to reconcile conflicting Content-Length...
description: Tinyproxy through 1.11.3, fixed in commit ff45d3b, fails to reconcile conflicting Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding: chunked headers, forwarding both verbatim to the backend while using Content-Length to determine how many request body bytes to consume. Remote attackers can desynchronize the proxy and backend parser state, allowing injection of arbitrary HTTP requests to the backend to enable cache poisoning, access control bypass, and request hijacking.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54387.

title: Typemill before 2.24.0 contains a path traversal vulnerability that allows authenticated...
description: Typemill before 2.24.0 contains a path traversal vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers with Author-level privileges to read arbitrary files outside the content directory by supplying traversal sequences in the path query parameter passed to Storage::getFile() with an empty folder argument. Attackers can bypass traversal-prevention controls in Storage::getFolderPath() to access sensitive files.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with Author-level privileges to exploit a path traversal flaw, potentially exposing sensitive files outside the intended directory. This could lead to unauthorized access to configuration files, credentials, or other critical data.
recommended_actions: Update Typemill to version 2.24.0 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict Author-level privileges to trusted users and audit existing accounts for unnecessary access.
Implement additional monitoring and logging to detect unauthorized file access attempts.

title: Evil-WinRM through 3.9, fixed in commit 6ecd570, contains a path traversal vulnerability in the...
description: Evil-WinRM through 3.9, fixed in commit 6ecd570, contains a path traversal vulnerability in the download_dir() function that allows a rogue or compromised remote Windows server to write files outside the intended download directory by returning filenames with traversal sequences from Get-ChildItem command output that are passed unsanitized to File.join(). Attackers controlling the remote server can exploit this to overwrite sensitive client-side files such as SSH authorized_keys or shell configu

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-55201.

title: libssh2 through 1.11.1, fixed in commit 7acf3df contains an out-of-bounds write vulnerability in...
description: libssh2 through 1.11.1, fixed in commit 7acf3df contains an out-of-bounds write vulnerability in ssh2_transport_read() that fails to enforce upper bounds on packet_length field. Remote attackers can send crafted SSH packets with excessively large packet_length values to corrupt heap memory and achieve remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in libssh2 allows remote attackers to exploit an out-of-bounds write, potentially leading to heap memory corruption and remote code execution. This could compromise the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of affected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately update libssh2 to a version containing the fix (commit 7acf3df or later).
Conduct a thorough review of systems and applications using libssh2 to identify and patch vulnerable instances.
Implement network monitoring and intrusion detection systems to identify and block malicious SSH traffic exploiting this vulnerability.

title: Tinyproxy through 1.11.3, fixed in commit 364cdb6, fails to reject requests containing multiple...
description: Tinyproxy through 1.11.3, fixed in commit 364cdb6, fails to reject requests containing multiple Content-Length headers with differing values, forwarding all duplicate headers to the backend while using the first value to determine how many request body bytes to consume. Remote attackers can desynchronize the proxy and backend parser state, allowing injection of arbitrary HTTP requests to the backend to enable cache poisoning, access control bypass, and request hijacking.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54388.

title: libssh2 through 1.11.1, fixed in commit 1762685, contains a pre-authentication denial of service...
description: libssh2 through 1.11.1, fixed in commit 1762685, contains a pre-authentication denial of service vulnerability in the SSH_MSG_EXT_INFO handler in src/packet.c that allows a malicious SSH server to cause a client CPU exhaustion loop by sending a crafted extension count value. A malicious server can set nr_extensions to 0xFFFFFFFF during key exchange, causing the client to spin in a tight CPU loop for over 60 seconds because return values from _libssh2_get_string() are unchecked and the session ti

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-55199.

title: Tinyproxy through 1.11.3, fixed in commit 09312a1, fails to properly validate the Host header...
description: Tinyproxy through 1.11.3, fixed in commit 09312a1, fails to properly validate the Host header during stathost detection, allowing unauthenticated attackers to access the stats page by injecting a matching Host header or bypass detection via port manipulation. Remote attackers can trigger unauthorized access to internal proxy statistics or misroute requests as transparent proxy connections to circumvent access controls.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-55202.

title: When NGINX Plus or NGINX Open Source is configured as the data plane for NGINX Gateway Fabric, an...
description: When NGINX Plus or NGINX Open Source is configured as the data plane for NGINX Gateway Fabric, an injection vulnerability exists in the NGINX configuration generator component of NGINX Gateway Fabric. User-supplied string values from the NginxProxy Custom Resource Definition (CRD) access log format setting are rendered directly into NGINX configuration templates without sanitization or escaping. An authenticated attacker with permission to create or modify these CRDs may craft values that inject

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with permissions to create or modify NginxProxy Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) to inject malicious code into NGINX configuration templates. This could lead to unauthorized code execution, configuration manipulation, or service disruption.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest patched version of NGINX Gateway Fabric, if available, to address the vulnerability.
Restrict permissions for creating or modifying NginxProxy CRDs to only trusted and essential users.
Implement input validation and sanitization to prevent malicious injection in configuration templates.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in SourceCodester CET Automated Grading System with AI...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in SourceCodester CET Automated Grading System with AI Predictive Analytics 1.0. Affected is an unknown function of the file /index.php of the component Student Self-Registration Endpoint. The manipulation leads to improper access controls. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12529.

title: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.368 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability in the...
description: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.368 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability in the get_profile_cookie() function that accepts unauthenticated profile names from the hermes_profile cookie. An authenticated attacker can forge the hermes_profile cookie value to bypass profile-scoped authorization checks and access sessions, files, and resources across different profiles.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53871.

title: When NGINX Gateway Fabric is configured using GRPCRoutes, an authenticated, remote attacker with...
description:
When NGINX Gateway Fabric is configured using GRPCRoutes, an authenticated, remote attacker with permission to create or modify GRPCRoute resources can cause the NGINX Gateway Fabric control plane to terminate by sending undisclosed GRPCRoute configurations containing backendRef filters. 


Note: Software versions which have reached End of Technical Support (EoTS) are not evaluated.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: An authenticated, remote attacker can exploit this vulnerability to cause a denial-of-service (DoS) condition by terminating the NGINX Gateway Fabric control plane. This could disrupt critical services relying on GRPCRoutes, leading to downtime and potential loss of business operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to a patched version of NGINX Gateway Fabric that addresses CVE-2026-32682, if available.
Restrict permissions for creating or modifying GRPCRoute resources to only trusted and essential users.
Monitor and audit GRPCRoute configurations for any suspicious or unauthorized changes.

title: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.443 contains a broken access control vulnerability in the /api/session...
description: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.443 contains a broken access control vulnerability in the /api/session endpoint that allows authenticated users to disclose cross-profile session transcripts. Attackers can bypass profile boundary checks by directly querying session IDs belonging to other profiles via GET /api/session?session_id=<foreign_id>&messages=1 to retrieve unauthorized conversation transcripts and metadata.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users to bypass access controls and retrieve unauthorized session transcripts and metadata across user profiles. This could lead to data breaches, privacy violations, and potential exposure of sensitive or confidential information.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Hermes WebUI to version 0.51.443 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Conduct a review of access control mechanisms to ensure proper enforcement of profile boundaries and session validation.
Monitor logs for any unauthorized access attempts or unusual activity related to the /api/session endpoint.

title:
Use of an incorrectly resolved name or reference in the pinget backend 
in Devolutions UniGetUI...
description:
Use of an incorrectly resolved name or reference in the pinget backend 
in Devolutions UniGetUI 2026.2.0 and earlier allows a WinGet community 
catalog contributor to cause an installed application to be correlated 
to an unrelated, attacker-controlled catalog package and to execute an 
attacker-controlled installer via a crafted catalog package whose 
normalized name is contained as a substring within the installed 
application name when a user applies the proposed update.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10696.

title: Pimcore CMS/DXP version 12.3.8 contains a sandbox bypass vulnerability that allows authenticated...
description: Pimcore CMS/DXP version 12.3.8 contains a sandbox bypass vulnerability that allows authenticated administrative attackers to execute arbitrary methods on PHP objects by exploiting empty checkMethodAllowed() and checkPropertyAllowed() implementations in the custom Twig SecurityPolicy. Attackers can supply malicious Twig templates through the DataObject ClassDefinition Layout\Text component to perform arbitrary file reads, execute arbitrary database queries, and potentially achieve remote code exe

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The sandbox bypass vulnerability in Pimcore CMS/DXP version 12.3.8 can allow authenticated administrative attackers to execute arbitrary PHP methods, potentially leading to unauthorized file access, database compromise, and remote code execution. This poses a significant risk to data confidentiality, integrity, and availability.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Pimcore CMS/DXP to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-11407.
Restrict administrative access to trusted users only and enforce strong authentication mechanisms such as MFA.
Conduct a security audit of the system to identify any signs of compromise and monitor logs for suspicious activity.

title: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.443 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability in the session export...
description: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.443 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability in the session export endpoint that allows authenticated users to access sessions from other profiles. The _handle_session_export handler in api/routes.py fails to verify active-profile ownership before serializing session data, enabling attackers to exfiltrate foreign session transcripts by guessing or knowing session identifiers.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The authorization bypass vulnerability in Hermes WebUI allows authenticated users to access and exfiltrate session data from other user profiles. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, privacy breaches, and potential data leaks.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Hermes WebUI to version 0.51.443 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Review and audit access logs to identify any unauthorized access to session data and take appropriate remediation steps.
Implement additional verification mechanisms to ensure proper ownership checks in sensitive endpoints.
